description: This figure presents first-principles density functional theory (DFT) calculated cohesive energy (E_coh, red square marker, left y-axis with unit eVВ·atomвЃ»В№) and formation enthalpy (О”H_r, blue square marker, right y-axis with unit eVВ·atomвЃ»В№) of Feв‚Ѓв‚‹в‚“Mnв‚“Al alloys, plotted against Mn content (x-axis ranging from 0 to 1). Both E_coh and О”H_r show a monotonic increasing trend (becoming less negative) as Mn content rises. The E_coh values of all studied alloys range from -6.947 eVВ·atomвЃ»В№ (at Mn content = 0, FeAl alloy) to -6.568 eVВ·atomвЃ»В№ (at Mn content = 1, MnAl alloy), while О”H_r values range from -0.391 eVВ·atomвЃ»В№ to -0.215 eVВ·atomвЃ»В№. All calculated values are negative, confirming all Feв‚Ѓв‚‹в‚“Mnв‚“Al alloys in the studied composition range are thermodynamically stable. The lowest, most negative О”H_r value for FeAl indicates it is the most thermodynamically stable alloy in this system.
